title: Slit-scan
goal: Offset each row in time to produce a time-smeared image.

steps.body:
Slit-scan photography exposes one column of film at a time while
the subject moves past. The result is "time smeared along the
vertical axis." We fake it by sampling our procedural scene at a
different time per row.

Each row's time is `t + y * 0.02` — the bottom of the image is
offset roughly 5 seconds behind the top. The "scene" is a simple
sin-wave color band; any procedural signal will show the slit-scan
smear.
steps.source:
local AssetService = game:GetService("AssetService")
local RunService = game:GetService("RunService")

local W, H = 256, 256
local label = script.Parent
local img = AssetService:CreateEditableImage({ Size = Vector2.new(W, H) })
local buf = buffer.create(W * H * 4)

local t = 0
RunService.Heartbeat:Connect(function(dt)
  t += dt
  local k = 0
  for y = 0, H - 1 do
    local rowT = t + y * 0.02
    for x = 0, W - 1 do
      local fx = x / W
      local r = math.sin(fx * 10 + rowT * 2) * 0.5 + 0.5
      local g = math.sin(fx * 10 + rowT * 2 + 2) * 0.5 + 0.5
      local b = math.sin(fx * 10 + rowT * 2 + 4) * 0.5 + 0.5
      buffer.writeu8(buf, k, math.floor(r * 255))
      buffer.writeu8(buf, k + 1, math.floor(g * 255))
      buffer.writeu8(buf, k + 2, math.floor(b * 255))
      buffer.writeu8(buf, k + 3, 255)
      k += 4
    end
  end
  img:WritePixelsBuffer(Vector2.zero, img.Size, buf)
end)

label.ImageContent = Content.fromObject(img)

steps.body:
Press ▶ **Play** (F5). Diagonal bands flow across the canvas —
what was happening at the top a moment ago is now visible lower
down. Shift+F5 to stop.
